groupings.title: SEO/GEO (16) — research → build → optimize → monitor
groupings.description: Search & generative-engine optimization: keyword/SERP/gap research, content drafting and GEO citation-readiness, schema & page plays, then two auditor gates — CORE-EEAT (content-quality-auditor) and CITE (domain-authority-auditor) — plus rank/performance/off-site monitoring.

groupings.title: Influencer marketing (16) — discover → plan → activate → measure
groupings.description: Creator campaigns end to end: audience/niche mapping, trend and creator discovery with fit scoring, campaign planning/briefs/budgets, outreach and the C³ ART compliance gate (content-reviewer), contracts, amplification, and post-click/ROI measurement.

groupings.title: Paid ads · ROAS (16) — research → orchestrate → activate → scale
groupings.description: The ROAS loop: campaign architecture, audience segments, search-term mining and feed optimization; creative, tests, bids and landing checks; the RQS launch gate (ad-account-auditor) with conversion-signal QA; then measurement, attribution reconciliation, pacing and fatigue control.

groupings.title: Email · SEND (16) — setup → engage → nurture → deliver
groupings.description: Email programs for B2C lifecycle, B2B outbound, and newsletters: deliverability/auth pre-flight, list growth/segments/hygiene, creative and rendering, lifecycle sequences and monetization, then the EQS pre-send gate (email-quality-auditor), send experiments and inbox-placement monitoring.

groupings.title: Launch · RAMP (16) — research → assemble → mobilize → prove
groupings.description: Product launches for B2B SaaS, dev-tool/community, and mobile app-store use cases: positioning and launch tiering with a risk register, launch windows and early-access ladders, message house and press kits, pricing/packaging and sales enablement, then the LQS readiness gate (launch-readiness-auditor) with a T-1 go/no-go, launch-day runbook, community/press execution, launch-window monitoring, feedback triage, retro, and momentum planning.

groupings.title: Protocol layer (6) — shared truth registries & memory
groupings.description: Cross-discipline machinery the other 80 skills read from: canonical entity profiles, the creator roster, offer/claim substantiation, per-subject consent/suppression records, the canonical launch dossier/calendar, and the campaign memory loop. Anything below this section is a legacy pre-v12 skill name kept only for historical installs.
